title: Mentha parietariifolia
intro: Mentha parietariifolia, also known as False Horsemint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, providing relief from headaches, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Mentha parietariifolia include menthone, menthyl acetate, and rosmarinic acid, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Mentha parietariifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Mentha, commonly known as mint. Within the genus, Mentha parietariifolia is considered a member of the Mentha section. The species is native to parts of Europe, where it grows in damp environments. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

benefits: Mentha parietariifolia, commonly known as the wall balm mint, has been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
